titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Reserve Officers Training Corps, Yale University, Records
Reserve Officers Training Corps, Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday January 6, 2019 at 09:28
finding aid revisiion description not supplied

did.unittitle: Reserve Officers Training Corps, Yale University, records
did.abstract: The records consist of correspondence, reports, memoranda, photographs, newspaper clippings, and memorabilia documenting the activities and operations of the Army and Navy Reserve Officers Training Corps (ROTC) at Yale.

corpname.part: Yale University. Reserve Officers' Training Corps

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 42-A (YRG 42-A), Records of military training programs at Yale.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Reserve Officers Training Corps, Yale University, Records (RU 599). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by type of material.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Yale Battery was the first Army training program established at Yale in 1915. Eventually Yale had four batteries which became the Connecticut 10th Field Artillery of the National Guard. In 1916, Yale replaced its batteries with the Reserve Officers Training Corps (ROTC) and the Yale Naval Training Unit (originally the Motor Boat Patrol and later known as NROTC). In the spring of 1918, existing ROTC programs were suspended and replaced with the Student Army Training Corps (SATC). The program was designed to establish military training units in colleges and to substitute specialized military training for the normal curriculum during World War I. Students were subject to military regulations and the existing ROTC programs (Army and Naval) were coordinated by the SATC. The SATC was demobilized by Congress in 1918. The ROTC programs were re-established at Yale in 1919.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of correspondence, reports, memoranda, photographs, newspaper clippings, and memorabilia documenting the activities and operations of the Army and Navy Reserve Officers Training Corps (ROTC) at Yale.

corpname.part: United States. Army. Reserve Officers' Training Corps
United States. Naval Reserve Officers Training Corps
Yale University. Naval Reserve Officers Training Corps
